researched_category.notes: Snowflake is a cloud-based AI data platform (data warehouse and analytics) that provides storage, compute, and AI/ML services for enterprise data.

one_liner.value: Cloud-based AI data platform for storing, processing, and analyzing structured and semi-structured data at scale with built-in AI/ML capabilities.

methods.notes: Snowflake REST APIs support: Key Pair authentication (JWT), OAuth 2.0 (Snowflake OAuth and External OAuth), Programmatic Access Tokens (PAT - bearer tokens), and Workload Identity Federation. The SQL API also supports OAuth and Key Pair auth. PAT is the simplest for agent toolkits.

access.notes: Free trial requires only a valid email address (work email requested but not enforced). No credit card required. 30-day trial with $400 in free credits. API access is available on the free trial. After trial, convert to self-serve paid (on-demand) account with credit card. No admin approval needed.

summary.value: Snowflake has two main REST API surfaces: (1) Snowflake REST APIs for resource management - covers accounts, users, roles, warehouses, databases, schemas, tables, views, stages, streams, tasks, security policies, AI/ML (Cortex Embed, Cortex Inference, Cortex Search), notebooks, Streamlit, and more. OpenAPI specs available on GitHub. (2) Snowflake SQL API - RESTful interface for executing SQL statements (queries, DDL, DML). Both are well-documented and actively maintained.

agent_interface.notes: Snowflake has an official managed MCP server (public preview Oct 2025, GA Nov 2025) that exposes Cortex Analyst, Cortex Search, Cortex Agents, SQL execution, and custom UDFs/stored procedures as MCP tools. Authentication via OAuth or PAT. Community MCP servers also exist (e.g., davidamom/snowflake-mcp on GitHub).

rationale.value: Snowflake is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has well-documented public REST APIs (resource management + SQL API), multiple auth methods suitable for programmatic use (PAT, OAuth, key pair JWT), self-serve free trial access with no credit card required, and an official Snowflake-managed MCP server that is GA. The API surface is broad, covering data warehousing, AI/ML, security, and resource management. No material blockers exist for building an agent toolkit.

s1.quote: Snowflake REST APIs for resource management provide a set of endpoints that lets users programmatically interact with and control various resources within the Snowflake Data Cloud.
s1.title: Snowflake REST APIs

s2.quote: This topic describes how to authenticate to the server when using the Snowflake REST APIs. When you send a request, the request must include authentication information using either of the following: Using key pair authentication, Using OAuth, Using a programmatic access token (PAT), Using workload identity federation
s2.title: Authenticating Snowflake REST APIs

s3.quote: When you send a request, the request must include authentication information. ... Using OAuth ... Using key-pair authentication
s3.title: Authenticating to the server (SQL API)

s4.quote: A Snowflake trial account lets you evaluate/test Snowflake's full range of innovative and powerful features with no cost or contractual obligations. To sign up for a trial account, all you need is a valid email address; no payment information or other qualifying information is required.
s4.title: Trial accounts

s5.quote: Start your 30-day trial with $400 in free credits ... First name, Last name, Work email, Company name, Job title
s5.title: Snowflake Trial Signup

s6.quote: We keep pricing simple with a consumption-based pricing model. Explore the details of our pricing model and how you can easily understand costs.
s6.title: Pricing Options

s7.quote: The Snowflake-managed MCP server lets AI agents securely retrieve data from Snowflake accounts without needing to deploy separate infrastructure.
s7.title: Snowflake-managed MCP server

s8.quote: Turn data and AI into impact with developer guides, hands-on labs and more. Get started your way with two Snowflake trial options: Unlock the full Snowflake AI Data Cloud with $400 in free credits
s8.title: Snowflake Trial

s9.quote: We are excited to announce the availability of Snowflake's managed Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in public preview, giving AI agents an open-standards-based interface to connect with AI-ready data in Snowflake.
s9.title: Introducing Snowflake Managed MCP Servers

s10.quote: Snowflake REST APIs provides the following APIs: Account API, Alert API, ... Database API, ... Table API, ... Warehouse API, ... Cortex Embed API, ... [40+ API categories]
s10.title: Snowflake REST APIs reference

s11.quote: The Snowflake SQL API is a REST API that you can use to access and update data in a Snowflake database. You can use this API to develop custom applications and integrations that: Perform queries, Manage your deployment
s11.title: Snowflake SQL API

s12.quote: MCP server implementation for Snowflake integration. This server implements the Model Context Protocol to allow any MCP client to: Execute SQL queries on Snowflake databases
s12.title: GitHub - davidamom/snowflake-mcp
